artifact.content_preview:
From: j.henderson@techsupplier.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: bob.morrison@ssc-spc.gc.ca; ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 09:14:32 -0500

Alice,

Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation
with the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre
migration.

Unfortunately, our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse
management system failure that has affected ou

extracted_tasks.text: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches pushes arrival to March 20, after the March 8 migration window opens, directly threatening the Q2 migration schedule.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line creates a critical supply chain vulnerability with no viable rapid alternative; Vancouver routing adds a further 3–5 business days of delay.

evidence_refs.excerpt: we are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: Insufficient staging and testing time: Alice's email indicates equipment must arrive by March 6 for staging and testing before the March 8 migration window. Even if delivery occurs March 20, there is zero buffer for pre-migration validation, risking undetected hardware or configuration failures entering production.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our migration window opens March 8 and we need the equipment on site no later than March 6 for staging and testing.

risks.description: Cascading Q2 budget risk: a forced delay to the migration window may trigger contract penalty clauses, emergency procurement costs, extended resource bookings, or overtime charges that were not budgeted for the NGIS Phase 3 project.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at TechSupplier's distribution centre, suggesting an ongoing and potentially unresolved IT instability that could cause further shipment disruptions beyond the currently stated March 20 date.

evidence_refs.excerpt: our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.
